verses.text: Mary anoints Jesus' feet with costly spikenard; the house is filled with the fragrance. Lavish love. She pours out her best upon the Lord.
Mary anoints Jesus' feet with costly spikenard; the house is filled with the fragrance. Lavish love. She pours out her best upon the Lord.
Mary anoints Jesus' feet with costly spikenard; the house is filled with the fragrance. Lavish love. She pours out her best upon the Lord.
Judas objects, why not sold for the poor? he said this 'being a thief.' False care. He cloaks greed in concern for the poor.
Judas objects, why not sold for the poor? he said this 'being a thief.' False care. He cloaks greed in concern for the poor.
Judas objects, why not sold for the poor? he said this 'being a thief.' False care. He cloaks greed in concern for the poor.
Let her alone; she keeps it 'against the day of my burying'; the poor ye have always. Honoured devotion. Christ defends the love others despise.
Let her alone; she keeps it 'against the day of my burying'; the poor ye have always. Honoured devotion. Christ defends the love others despise.
Crowds come to see Lazarus; the priests plot to kill him too, for many believed. Malice grows. They would silence even the proof of His power.
Crowds come to see Lazarus; the priests plot to kill him too, for many believed. Malice grows. They would silence even the proof of His power.
Crowds come to see Lazarus; the priests plot to kill him too, for many believed. Malice grows. They would silence even the proof of His power.
The multitude meet Him with palms, 'Hosanna: Blessed is the King'; He sits on an ass's colt. Lowly King. He enters in the meekness foretold.
The multitude meet Him with palms, 'Hosanna: Blessed is the King'; He sits on an ass's colt. Lowly King. He enters in the meekness foretold.
The multitude meet Him with palms, 'Hosanna: Blessed is the King'; He sits on an ass's colt. Lowly King. He enters in the meekness foretold.
The multitude meet Him with palms, 'Hosanna: Blessed is the King'; He sits on an ass's colt. Lowly King. He enters in the meekness foretold.
The disciples understood later; the Pharisees say, 'the world is gone after him.' Grudging witness. Even His foes own His drawing power.
The disciples understood later; the Pharisees say, 'the world is gone after him.' Grudging witness. Even His foes own His drawing power.
The disciples understood later; the Pharisees say, 'the world is gone after him.' Grudging witness. Even His foes own His drawing power.
The disciples understood later; the Pharisees say, 'the world is gone after him.' Grudging witness. Even His foes own His drawing power.
Certain Greeks desire to see Jesus; Philip and Andrew bring word. Nations seeking. The Gentiles begin to turn to Christ.
Certain Greeks desire to see Jesus; Philip and Andrew bring word. Nations seeking. The Gentiles begin to turn to Christ.
Certain Greeks desire to see Jesus; Philip and Andrew bring word. Nations seeking. The Gentiles begin to turn to Christ.
'Except a corn of wheat fall into the ground and die, it abideth alone'; he that loves his life shall lose it. Death for fruit. His death, like the buried seed, brings a great harvest.
'Except a corn of wheat fall into the ground and die, it abideth alone'; he that loves his life shall lose it. Death for fruit. His death, like the buried seed, brings a great harvest.
'Except a corn of wheat fall into the ground and die, it abideth alone'; he that loves his life shall lose it. Death for fruit. His death, like the buried seed, brings a great harvest.
'Except a corn of wheat fall into the ground and die, it abideth alone'; he that loves his life shall lose it. Death for fruit. His death, like the buried seed, brings a great harvest.
'Now is my soul troubled'; Father, glorify thy name; a voice answers, I have, and will. Resolved obedience. He shrinks, yet seeks the Father's glory.
'Now is my soul troubled'; Father, glorify thy name; a voice answers, I have, and will. Resolved obedience. He shrinks, yet seeks the Father's glory.
The people think it thundered; 'this voice came... for your sakes.' Heaven's witness. The Father speaks to confirm the Son.
The people think it thundered; 'this voice came... for your sakes.' Heaven's witness. The Father speaks to confirm the Son.
'If I be lifted up... will draw all men unto me'; He spoke of His death. Drawing cross. The crucified Christ draws the world to Himself.
'If I be lifted up... will draw all men unto me'; He spoke of His death. Drawing cross. The crucified Christ draws the world to Himself.
'If I be lifted up... will draw all men unto me'; He spoke of His death. Drawing cross. The crucified Christ draws the world to Himself.
Walk while ye have the light, 'that ye may be the children of light.' Urgent call. The day of grace will not last for ever.
Walk while ye have the light, 'that ye may be the children of light.' Urgent call. The day of grace will not last for ever.
Walk while ye have the light, 'that ye may be the children of light.' Urgent call. The day of grace will not last for ever.
Though He did many miracles, they believed not, fulfilling Isaiah, 'who hath believed our report?' Judicial blindness. Persistent unbelief is at last given over to itself.
Though He did many miracles, they believed not, fulfilling Isaiah, 'who hath believed our report?' Judicial blindness. Persistent unbelief is at last given over to itself.
Though He did many miracles, they believed not, fulfilling Isaiah, 'who hath believed our report?' Judicial blindness. Persistent unbelief is at last given over to itself.
Though He did many miracles, they believed not, fulfilling Isaiah, 'who hath believed our report?' Judicial blindness. Persistent unbelief is at last given over to itself.
Many rulers believed but feared to confess, loving 'the praise of men more than the praise of God.' Secret faith. The fear of man stifles open confession.
Many rulers believed but feared to confess, loving 'the praise of men more than the praise of God.' Secret faith. The fear of man stifles open confession.
Many rulers believed but feared to confess, loving 'the praise of men more than the praise of God.' Secret faith. The fear of man stifles open confession.
He that believes on Me believes on Him that sent Me; I am come a light. Sent of God. To trust Christ is to trust the Father who sent Him.
He that believes on Me believes on Him that sent Me; I am come a light. Sent of God. To trust Christ is to trust the Father who sent Him.
He that believes on Me believes on Him that sent Me; I am come a light. Sent of God. To trust Christ is to trust the Father who sent Him.
I came not to judge but to save; the word I have spoken 'shall judge him in the last day.' The word as judge. What men do with His word decides their end.
I came not to judge but to save; the word I have spoken 'shall judge him in the last day.' The word as judge. What men do with His word decides their end.
